content:
I got this warning, and I'm so confused. A reference is not interior mutable. Yes, the pointee is, but the pointee is in a static.

warning: a `const` item should not be interior mutable
   --> /usr/local/google/home/aliceryhl/devel/linux/drivers/android/binder/thread.rs:436:1
    |
436 | const THREAD_CONDVAR_CLASS: Pin<&'static LockClassKey> = kernel::static_lock_class!();
    | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    |
    = help: consider making this a static item
    = help: for further information visit https://rust-lang.github.io/rust-clippy/master/index.html#declare_interior_mutable_const
    = note: `-W clippy::declare-interior-mutable-const` implied by `-W clippy::all`
    = help: to override `-W clippy::all` add `#[allow(clippy::declare_interior_mutable_const)]`

warning: 1 warning emitted

Oh I guess there is a bug for this:
https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/issues/13233

Also, 1.83.0 triggers this:

error[E0080]: it is undefined behavior to use this value
   --> /home/runner/work/linux/linux/linux/drivers/android/binder/thread.rs:436:1
    |
436 | const THREAD_CONDVAR_CLASS: Pin<&'static LockClassKey> = kernel::static_lock_class!();
    | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ constructing invalid value at .__pointer: encountered reference to mutable memory in `const`
    |
    = note: The rules on what exactly is undefined behavior aren't clear, so this check might be overzealous. Please open an issue on the rustc repository if you believe it should not be considered undefined behavior.
    = note: the raw bytes of the constant (size: 8, align: 8) {
                ╾──────alloc1315──────╼                         │ ╾──────╼
            }
the error seems to be from miri/const eval, did you file a bug?
I see, not sure if it's possible to work around the error
Is this an error even with const_mut_refs?
